title: Preparation of 0.2M EDTA, pH 8.0
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"di4m4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"45bku","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"3777o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dvnjv","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"1122f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d0frm","text":"Purpose of examination / Clinical relevance","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3oi9l","text":"At the end of 2019, several pneumonia cases were reported in Wuhan, China and the pathogen was confirmed as a new viral strain. World Health organization has named the newly identified coronavirus as 2019-nCoV, also known as COVID19. The disease developed into a dangerous pandemic, posing major challenges to the NHS. Although more research is necessary to better understand the virus, in response to the emergency, simple and rapid testing is essential to identify the virus in infected individuals. This will aid the implementation of efficient interventions to contain the spread, and distinguish healthcare workers who have been infected, and are required to self-isolate, from those showing similar symptoms but which are not 2019-nCoV associated. The latter category may continue to work, alleviating stress on hard-pressed healthcare resources. 2019-nCoV is an RNA virus, and the diagnostic tests detect viral RNA in swabs from patient airways using a reverse transcriptase PCR assay. Samples are submitted to HSL, an accredited reporting laboratory, and transferred to the Crick for testing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"t9ds","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dg6p4","text":"Principles of Examination","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"co0m3","text":"This procedure involves the preparation of 0.2M EDTA pH 8.0 for making L6 virus inactivation buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":71,"length":28}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"length":38,"offset":29}],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"3c84p","text":"This protocol is part of the Crick COVID-19 RT-PCR Testing Pipeline collection.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{"0":{"data":{"guid":"B8CDFCD096F611EABF8D3BD97AAE438C","url":"https://www.protocols.io/private/9BA5EEFF847911EA9B0C0242AC110002"},"mutability":"","type":"link"}}}},"1":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":101,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"33p8b","text":"THIS DOCUMENT MUST BE READ AND UNDERSTOOD BY STAFF USING IT AND DOCUMENTED EVIDENCE PROVIDED THEREOF.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"C5FEC1F096F611EABF8D3BD97AAE438C","url":"https://www.protocols.io/private/D1949D20846811EA9B0C0242AC110002"}}}}

If a spill does occur follow the procedure within the spill kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Please select between the recipes for 1 L 0.2 M EDTA pH 8.0 and 2 L 0.2 M EDTA pH 8.0 . Work in fume hood!
Weigh out 74.45 g, EDTA disodium salt , dihydrate and add to a 1 L beaker.
Measure out 800 mL, MilliQ water and add to the beaker.
Add a magnetic flea and place on a magnetic stirring plate to mix the solution. The EDTA salt will not go into solution until the pH reaches pH 8.0 .
Add a newly calibrated pH meter into the solution to observe the pH.

To dissolve the salt, add sodium hydroxide (NaOH) pellets to the solution. Add a few pellets at a time and wait until the pellets have fully dissolved before adding more. It may take around 8 g, NaOH pellets before the pH is at pH 8.0 .
Fine tune pH with 5 Molarity (M), NaOH .
Once fully dissolved (this will take some time so be patient), transfer to 1 L beaker and top up the solution to 1 L using MilliQ water , if necessary.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store 0.2 Molarity (M), EDTA pH 8.0 solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
Weigh out 148.89 g, EDTA disodium salt , dihydrate and add to a 2 L beaker.
Measure out 1600 mL, MilliQ water and add to the beaker.
Add a magnetic flea and place on a magnetic stirring plate to mix the solution. The EDTA salt will not go into solution until the pH reaches pH 8.0 .
Add a newly calibrated pH meter into the solution to observe the pH.

To dissolve the salt, add sodium hydroxide (NaOH) pellets to the solution. Add a few pellets at a time and wait until the pellets have fully dissolved before adding more. It may take around 16 g, NaOH pellets before the pH is at pH 8.0 .
Fine tune pH with 5 Molarity (M), NaOH .
Once fully dissolved (this will take some time so be patient), transfer to 2 L beaker and top up the solution to 2 L using MilliQ water , if necessary.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store 0.2 Molarity (M), EDTA pH 8.0 solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
creator: Jerome Nicod
